diagrams.title: Electromagnetic Spectrum
diagrams.description: Shows frequency ranges from radio waves to gamma rays with applications
elements: Radio waves (< 3 GHz): Radio, TV, Mobile
Infrared (300 GHz - 400 THz): Remote controls, Fiber optics
diagrams.purpose: Understand frequency bands and their applications in wireless communication

diagrams.title: Signal Propagation Types
diagrams.description: Three main propagation modes with path illustrations
elements: Ground wave: Follows Earth's curvature, low frequency (< 3 MHz)
Sky wave: Reflects off ionosphere, medium frequency (3-30 MHz)
Space wave: Line-of-sight, high frequency (> 30 MHz)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how radio waves travel from transmitter to receiver in different frequency ranges

diagrams.title: Modulation Types Comparison
diagrams.description: AM and FM waveform comparisons showing carrier and information signals
elements: Modulating signal: Information to be transmitted
diagrams.purpose: Understand difference between amplitude and frequency modulation techniques

diagrams.title: Wireless Communication System Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Basic components of wireless transmitter and receiver
diagrams.purpose: Understand functional blocks in wireless communication systems

diagrams.title: Antenna Radiation Patterns
diagrams.description: Comparison of isotropic, omnidirectional, and directional antenna patterns
elements: Omnidirectional: Doughnut shape in 3D (horizontal all directions)
Directional (Yagi): Focused beam in one direction
Parabolic: Very narrow, high gain beam
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how different antennas distribute transmitted energy
